meta.title: A Life in Games
meta.subtitle: every game I can remember playing — and the ones still waiting for me

games.name: Marvel's Spider-Man
games.description: You swing through an open-world New York City as Peter Parker/Spider-Man, using web-swinging as the main way to get around and combat that mixes acrobatic melee hits, web gadgets, and stealth takedowns. Between story missions you clean up crimes and do side content across the map.

games.name: Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales
games.description: A smaller, standalone follow-up where you play Miles Morales instead of Peter Parker, swinging around a snowy Harlem/Manhattan during a winter holiday setting. Combat adds Miles's unique bio-electric 'Venom' shock powers and a camouflage/invisibility ability on top of web-swinging and gadgets.

games.name: God of War III
games.description: You play Kratos climbing Mount Olympus to take revenge on the Greek gods, fighting through giant boss encounters against figures like Poseidon, Hades, and Zeus. Combat centers on chained blade weapons, magic abilities, and brutal context-sensitive finishing sequences. Originally a PS3 game; on PS4 it's played via the 2015 remaster.

games.name: God of War
games.description: A reboot of the series moving Kratos into Norse mythology, playing alongside his young son Atreus as a companion who fights and gives advice throughout. The camera sits close over Kratos's shoulder in a continuous, unbroken shot, and combat is built around a throwable/recallable axe plus hand-to-hand strikes.

games.name: God of War Ragnarök
games.description: The direct sequel to 2018's God of War, continuing Kratos and Atreus's story as the Norse prophesied Ragnarok approaches. You fight using the returning axe alongside Kratos's classic chained blades and a shield, and travel between multiple Norse realms that are more open to explore.

games.name: Uncharted: Drake's Fortune
games.description: The first Uncharted game, playing treasure hunter Nathan Drake through jungle ruins searching for the lost treasure of El Dorado. Gameplay alternates between cover-based gunfights, climbing/platforming across ledges and cliffs, and light environmental puzzle solving. On PS4, played via the 2015 Nathan Drake Collection remaster.

games.name: Uncharted 2: Among Thieves
games.description: Nathan Drake searches for the lost city of Shambhala and the Cintamani Stone, working through snowy mountain villages, a moving train, and ruined temples. Refines the first game's cover shooting, ledge-climbing traversal, and set-piece action. On PS4, played via the 2015 Nathan Drake Collection remaster.

games.name: Uncharted 3: Drake's Deception
games.description: Nathan Drake chases the legend of the lost Arabian city Ubar, with a plot tied to his mentor Sully and a rivalry with antagonist Katherine Marlowe. Same core mix of cover-based gunfights, climbing, and puzzle-solving, with set pieces like a sinking cruise ship. On PS4, played via the 2015 Nathan Drake Collection remaster.

games.name: Uncharted 4: A Thief's End
games.description: A native PS4 game and the last mainline entry starring Nathan Drake, pulled out of retirement by his previously unmentioned brother Sam to search for the pirate treasure of Henry Avery. Keeps cover shooting and climbing but adds a rope/grapple hook and larger exploration areas.

games.name: Uncharted: The Lost Legacy
games.description: A standalone, shorter Uncharted game where you play Chloe Frazer, teamed up with Nadine Ross, searching for the Tusk of Ganesh in India amid a regional conflict. Reuses Uncharted 4's mechanics and includes one notably large, semi-open hub area to explore by jeep.

games.name: WWE 2K19
games.description: A wrestling sim with the full WWE roster, where matches are controlled through grapple/strike/reversal timing rather than fighting-game-style combos. You can build a custom wrestler through MyCareer, or use create-a-wrestler/create-a-arena tools alongside standard exhibition matches.

games.name: Cricket 19
games.description: A licensed cricket sim from Big Ant Studios built around the 2019 Ashes series, letting you play international test, ODI, and T20 matches with manual timing-based batting and bowling controls, plus a career mode building a custom player.

games.name: Detroit: Become Human
games.description: A branching, choice-driven story game where you alternate control between three androids in a near-future Detroit where androids serve as household labor. Gameplay is mostly quick-time-event action sequences and dialogue/action choices that alter a visible branching flowchart of outcomes.

games.name: Realm Royale Reforged
games.description: A class-based battle royale from Hi-Rez Studios where you pick a fantasy class with its own abilities, and craft weapons at forges scattered across the map instead of finding all your gear on the ground. Eliminated players turn into a chicken that must survive and regear before dying for good.

games.name: Titanfall 2
games.description: A fast-movement FPS where pilots wall-run, double-jump, and slide through levels on foot, then call in and pilot large mech-like Titans for heavier combat. Has a well-regarded single-player campaign plus a separate multiplayer mode mixing pilot and Titan combat.

games.name: Batman: Arkham Knight
games.description: The final entry in the Arkham series, set in an open-world Gotham City you can traverse by grappling between rooftops or driving the drivable Batmobile. Gameplay combines free-flow melee combat, predator-style stealth takedowns, and detective-mode investigation.

games.name: Fall Guys
games.description: Up to 60 players in bean-shaped costumed characters race and stumble through obstacle-course minigames, with each round eliminating players until a small group remains for a final game. Purely physical-comedy platforming and light physics-based chaos rather than combat.

games.name: Spellbreak
games.description: A battle royale where guns are replaced by elemental spellcasting gauntlets (fire, ice, lightning, poison, stone, and wind), each with its own primary attack, secondary ability, and combo interactions with other elements. You loot gauntlets, runes, and gear across a shrinking map.

games.name: Grand Theft Auto V
games.description: You switch between three playable protagonists across a fictionalized Los Angeles and surrounding countryside, driving, shooting, and pulling off scripted heists that you can approach with different strategies and crews. Also includes GTA Online, a persistent multiplayer mode.

games.name: Rogue Company
games.description: A hero-based, third-person PvP shooter where each playable 'Rogue' has a unique loadout of weapons and a special gadget/ability. Matches are objective- or elimination-based team modes on relatively small maps.

games.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2
games.description: The original 2009 sequel to Call of Duty 4, continuing the story of Task Force 141 and Soap MacTavish through a globe-trotting military campaign. Multiplayer introduced popular additions like killstreak rewards and reworked perks on fast-paced small-to-medium maps. This is the older game, distinct from the 2022 sequel of the same subtitle.

games.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare
games.description: A 2019 reboot of the Modern Warfare sub-series with a grittier, more grounded tone and rebuilt cast, featuring a single-player campaign plus multiplayer and a cooperative Spec Ops mode. This game's engine/systems later became the base for the Warzone battle royale mode.

games.name: Street Fighter V
games.description: A traditional 2D-plane fighting game from Capcom with a roster of Street Fighter characters, each with special moves, a super meter, and the series' V-Skill/V-Trigger mechanics unique to this entry. You play through story mode, arcade-style ladders, and versus matches.

games.name: A Way Out
games.description: A story-driven game designed to be played entirely in two-player co-op, following two convicts, Leo and Vincent, as they plan and execute a prison escape and what follows afterward. Gameplay shifts between stealth, action sequences, quick-time events, and small minigames.

games.name: Mortal Kombat 11
games.description: A 2D-plane fighting game with the Mortal Kombat cast, known for its brutal special finishing moves (Fatalities) and a time-travel-themed story mode that revisits earlier games' characters and events. Includes the Krypt, an explorable 3D hub area for unlocking cosmetics.

games.name: Splitgate
games.description: An arena FPS that combines Halo-style gunplay and arena maps with a Portal-style mechanic: you can place linked entry/exit portals on marked surfaces to instantly traverse the map, flank enemies, or escape fights. Played in team-based multiplayer modes on compact, vertical maps.

games.name: Call of Duty: Vanguard
games.description: A World War II military shooter with a story campaign following soldiers across different fronts, plus the usual multiplayer and Zombies modes. You run through linear combat missions and also drop into fast, arcade-style online matches with classic COD weapon loadouts and killstreaks.

games.name: Call of Duty: WWII
games.description: A back-to-basics World War II shooter with a campaign following a US Army squad from D-Day onward, emphasizing traditional infantry combat. Multiplayer brought back grounded gunplay and introduced the social Headquarters hub area between matches, plus a co-op Nazi zombies mode.

games.name: Watch Dogs 2
games.description: An open-world game set in a fictionalized San Francisco Bay Area where you play a hacker who breaks into the city's connected systems to pull off heists and expose a surveillance company. You hack cameras, cars, and phones to solve problems in stealthy or chaotic ways.

games.name: Dragon Ball FighterZ
games.description: A 3v3 tag-team fighting game built around Dragon Ball characters, using 2.5D visuals designed to look like the anime in motion. You pick a team of three fighters, swap between them mid-combo, and pull off flashy combos, super moves, and dramatic finishes.

games.name: Dragon Ball Z: Kakarot
games.description: An action RPG that retells the Dragon Ball Z story from the Saiyan Saga through the Buu Saga, letting you fly around open areas between story fights. Combat is real-time melee and ki-blast fighting as Goku and other Z Fighters, mixed with light RPG elements like leveling and side activities.

games.name: Far Cry 6
games.description: An open-world FPS set on the fictional Caribbean island nation of Yara, where you play a guerrilla fighter working to overthrow a dictator. You explore a large open map, take over military bases, craft improvised weapons called 'supremo' backpacks, and mix stealth with loud firefights.

games.name: Ghost of Tsushima
games.description: An open-world samurai game set during the 13th-century Mongol invasion of Tsushima Island, where you play a samurai forced to adopt stealthier, less honorable tactics to fight back. You ride a horse across a vividly colored open world and fight in one-on-one sword duels or sneak through Mongol camps.

games.name: The Crew Motorfest
games.description: An open-world racing game set on a fictionalized version of Oahu, Hawaii, built around themed 'playlists' of races spanning street racing, off-road, drifting, and airplane and boat events. You collect and customize a garage of cars, motorcycles, boats, and planes.

games.name: Horizon Zero Dawn
games.description: An open-world action RPG set in a post-apocalyptic future overrun by robotic animals, where you play a hunter named Aloy uncovering the mystery of how the world ended up this way. You use a bow and other ranged weapons along with traps to bring down machines by targeting specific mechanical parts.

games.name: Horizon Forbidden West
games.description: The direct sequel to Horizon Zero Dawn, following Aloy as she travels west into a new, larger region full of new machine types and factions. Gameplay expands on the original with more weapon types, a diving/swimming mechanic, gliding, and an override tool for controlling machines.

games.name: Overwatch
games.description: A 6v6 team-based shooter where each match you pick from a roster of heroes with unique weapons and abilities, split across roles like damage, tank, and support. Matches revolve around objectives like escorting a payload or capturing points rather than simple deathmatch.

games.name: Apex Legends
games.description: A free-to-play battle royale shooter set in the Titanfall universe, where squads of three drop onto a shrinking map and fight to be the last team standing. You pick a Legend with unique abilities that add tactical options on top of standard gunplay.

games.name: Fortnite
games.description: A free-to-play battle royale where up to 100 players parachute onto an island, scavenge weapons and materials, and fight to be the last one standing as a storm shrinks the play area. A defining feature is building: you can instantly harvest wood, stone, and metal to throw up walls, ramps, and towers.

games.name: Rocket League
games.description: A sports-action game that's essentially soccer played with rocket-powered cars, where you drive into a giant ball to knock it into the opposing goal. Cars can boost, jump, and flip through the air to hit the ball acrobatically, and matches are quick, usually just a few minutes long.

games.name: It Takes Two
games.description: A two-player-only co-op game where a couple on the verge of divorce are magically turned into dolls and must work together to become human again. Each level introduces new mechanics and abilities that are split between the two characters, requiring constant communication.

games.name: Need for Speed Heat
games.description: An open-world racing game set in the fictional Palm City, where you earn cash from sanctioned daytime races and reputation from illegal nighttime street races, with the police escalating the more you push it at night. You buy, customize, and upgrade a garage of cars.

games.name: Brawlhalla
games.description: A free-to-play platform fighter in the style of Super Smash Bros, where you knock opponents off the stage rather than depleting a health bar. You pick from a large roster of characters, each with weapon-based movesets, and battle in free-for-all or team matches.

games.name: Stick Fight: The Game
games.description: A simple physics-based couch/online multiplayer game where stick figures duke it out on minimalist, hazard-filled stages using randomly spawned weapons. Controls are intentionally floppy and unpredictable, leading to chaotic, funny fights rather than precise competitive play.

games.name: The Last of Us
games.description: A story-driven survival action game set twenty years after a fungal outbreak has devastated society, following a smuggler named Joel escorting a teenage girl named Ellie across a ruined United States. You scavenge scarce resources to craft weapons and med kits, sneak past or fight infected and hostile survivors.

games.name: The Last of Us Part II
games.description: The sequel to The Last of Us, set several years later, following Ellie on a brutal journey of revenge after a violent event early in the game. Gameplay expands the original's stealth and scavenging combat with more open encounter spaces and multiple playable characters.

games.name: Star Wars Battlefront II
games.description: A Star Wars multiplayer shooter covering all three film eras, letting you play as Rebels, Empire, Resistance, or First Order troops, plus playable heroes and villains earned through in-match points. Also has a single-player campaign following an Imperial special forces commander.

games.name: Hitman 2
games.description: A stealth game where you play Agent 47, a professional assassin sent into large, open sandbox levels to eliminate specific targets using disguises, environmental kills, and careful planning. Each level can be approached many different ways, from loud action to elaborate 'accidental' deaths.

games.name: Call of Duty: Black Ops 4
games.description: A multiplayer-focused Call of Duty entry that dropped the traditional single-player campaign in favor of expanded multiplayer, a co-op Zombies mode with multiple maps, and Blackout, the series' first battle royale mode combining Black Ops characters, weapons, and locations into one large map.

games.name: Sleeping Dogs
games.description: An open-world crime game set in Hong Kong, where you play an undercover cop infiltrating a Triad gang, mixing martial arts combat, gunplay, and car chases through the city. Melee combat is a big focus, with fluid brawler-style hand-to-hand fighting alongside driving and shooting sections. On PS4, played via the 2014 Definitive Edition remaster.

games.name: Marvel's Spider-Man 2
games.description: You swing through an expanded New York City playing as both Peter Parker and Miles Morales, switching between them across the story and using each one's distinct powers in combat. Traversal adds web wings for gliding on top of the series' web-swinging, letting you cover the bigger map faster.

games.name: Paladins
games.description: You pick a champion from a roster of gunslingers, mages, and support characters, each with a unique weapon and set of abilities, then fight in 5v5 team objective matches like Siege or Onslaught. Between matches you build a deck of cards that passively boost your champion's abilities.

games.name: Watch Dogs
games.description: You play a vigilante hacker named Aiden Pearce moving through an open-world Chicago where almost every camera, traffic light, and phone can be hacked to your advantage. Missions mix driving, shooting, and stealth with environmental hacking puzzles, like raising bridges to escape police.

games.name: Saints Row IV
games.description: You lead the Saints, now the President of the United States, fighting off an alien invasion inside a simulated version of your own city. Combat combines guns with superpowers like super-speed, jumping, and telekinesis that make traversal and fights increasingly over-the-top.

games.name: Rainbow Six Siege
games.description: You pick an operator with a unique gadget, then attack or defend a small indoor map in short, tense rounds where walls, floors, and ceilings can be destroyed to create new sightlines and entry points. Round-based matches emphasize careful positioning and gadget play over fast reflexes alone.

games.name: Castle Crashers
games.description: Up to four players fight through a side-scrolling medieval kingdom, mashing buttons to combo enemies with swords, axes, and magic spells while leveling up a chosen knight. Loot dropped by enemies changes your character's weapon and animal companion, adding light RPG progression.

games.name: Broforce
games.description: You control a growing roster of parody action-movie heroes, running and gunning through side-scrolling levels that are almost entirely destructible, blowing tunnels through terrain with grenades and explosive barrels. Levels culminate in a countdown escape as the whole stage collapses around you.

games.name: Valorant
games.description: You pick an agent with unique abilities, then play 5v5 rounds planting or defusing a bomb-like Spike, similar in structure to Counter-Strike but with each agent's smokes, flashes, and other powers layered on top of precise gunplay. Economy management between rounds is as important as aim.

games.name: Cuphead
games.description: You play Cuphead or Mugman, running and jumping through side-scrolling stages that lead into elaborate boss fights against hand-drawn 1930s cartoon characters, each with multiple attack phases. The whole game is drawn and animated in the style of old rubber-hose cartoons.

games.name: Mortal Kombat 11
games.description: A 2D-plane fighting game with the Mortal Kombat cast, known for its brutal special finishing moves (Fatalities) and a time-travel-themed story mode that revisits earlier games' characters and events. Includes the Krypt, an explorable 3D hub area for unlocking cosmetics.

games.name: Dispatch
games.description: An episodic narrative game where you play a former superhero now managing a crew of misfit heroes-for-hire from a dispatch center, assigning them to incidents around the city and making dialogue choices that shape relationships and outcomes. Combat itself is mostly hands-off, with the drama coming from choices and consequences between missions.

games.name: Hades
games.description: You play Zagreus, son of the Greek god of the underworld, fighting your way up out of Hell through procedurally-arranged rooms of enemies using melee weapons, cast abilities, and boons granted by Olympian gods. Each failed attempt sends you back to the House of Hades, where story and permanent upgrades carry over.

games.name: Ghost of Yōtei
games.description: You play Atsu, a lone warrior seeking revenge across a fictionalized Mount Yotei region of Ezo (Hokkaido) in the 1600s, a standalone follow-up to Ghost of Tsushima using similar sword-dueling combat, stealth takedowns, and a wind-guided exploration system instead of map markers.

games.name: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
games.description: You lead a small expedition from the city of Lumière on a yearly mission to fight the Paintress, a godlike figure who erases everyone of a certain age from existence. Combat is turn-based but layered with real-time dodges, parries, and timed attacks, set in a painterly Belle Époque-inspired world.

games.name: Hollow Knight: Silksong
games.description: You play Hornet, exploring the kingdom of Pharloom as a nimble, agile hero with a needle and thread instead of the original Hollow Knight's nail and spells. It's a sprawling 2D Metroidvania of interconnected areas, tough boss fights, and gradually unlocked traversal abilities, faster-paced than its predecessor.

games.name: Hollow Knight
games.description: You explore Hallownest, a ruined, bug-inhabited underground kingdom, as a small silent knight fighting through 2D interconnected areas with a nail and collectible spells. Progress is built around finding new movement abilities like double jump and dash to reach previously blocked areas, in the classic Metroidvania mold.

games.name: Elden Ring
games.description: You explore the Lands Between, a large open world from FromSoftware, fighting tough enemies and bosses with weapons, spells, and careful stamina management, learning attack patterns through repeated deaths. Exploration is largely open and horse-mounted, with the story told obliquely through item descriptions and NPC quests.

games.name: Mortal Kombat 1
games.description: A soft reboot of the Mortal Kombat series set in a new timeline created by Fire God Liu Kang, with the same brutal Fatalities and combo-based one-on-one fighting as earlier games, plus a Kameo system letting you call in a second character for assist attacks mid-fight.

games.name: Black Myth: Wukong
games.description: You play the Destined One, a monkey-like warrior loosely based on Sun Wukong from Journey to the West, fighting through mythologically-inspired Chinese landscapes with a staff, transformations into defeated enemies' forms, and Souls-like stamina-based combat against large, elaborate bosses.

games.name: Dragon Ball: Sparking! Zero
games.description: You pick a fighter from a huge Dragon Ball roster and fight in wide-open 3D arenas that let you fly, dash, and clash in beam struggles, spiritual successor to the old Budokai Tenkaichi series. Story mode retells the anime's sagas with branching what-if paths, but most people play it for open dream matchups.

games.name: Saros
games.description: From the makers of Returnal, you fight through an alien world that resets every in-game day, permanently keeping some story progress and unlocks between runs while enemies and layouts change. Combat is a fast, bullet-hell-inflected third-person shooter with a growing arsenal of alien weapons.

games.name: Pragmata
games.description: Set on a near-future Moon base, you play Hugh, an astronaut-engineer paired with Diana, an android girl, fighting through the base together: Hugh handles direct combat while Diana hacks enemies and environmental puzzles in real time to open up new attacks and paths.

games.name: Tekken 8
games.description: A 3D fighting game where you sidestep in and out of the screen as well as block high/low, fighting one-on-one with each character's own deep move list of strings and launchers. Story mode continues the long-running Mishima family saga, alongside standard arcade and ranked online modes.

games.name: Street Fighter 6
games.description: A 2D-plane fighting game from Capcom with a mix of new and returning Street Fighter characters, featuring a Drive Gauge system that powers parries, dash cancels, and a temporary power-up burst. Includes a single-player World Tour mode with a custom avatar exploring a hub city, alongside standard versus and ranked modes.

games.name: Forza Horizon 6
games.description: An open-world arcade racer set in a new real-world-inspired location, where you drive a huge garage of licensed cars across open roads and off-road terrain, jumping between story-light 'Horizon' festival races, stunts, and photo-mode scenery. Continues the series' seasons/weather system and shared-world online exploration.

games.name: 007 First Light
games.description: An origin story for a young James Bond, from Hitman developer IO Interactive, mixing stealth infiltration, gadgets, and third-person shooting across globe-trotting missions. Levels are built with some of the same systemic, replayable design philosophy as the Hitman games, adapted to a more linear spy-thriller structure.

games.name: Dragon Ball Z: Budokai Tenkaichi 3
games.description: You pick a fighter from a huge roster of Dragon Ball characters and fight in wide-open 3D arenas that let you fly, dash behind opponents, and smash through mountains and buildings. Battles revolve around ki blasts, clashing beam struggles, and quick teleport-counters when someone rushes you.

games.name: Tom and Jerry in War of the Whiskers
games.description: You pick a cartoon character, Tom, Jerry, Spike, Butch, or others, and fight one-on-one in small destructible arenas like a kitchen or backyard. You punch, grab, and throw your opponent while using the environment against them, and fill up a Berserk meter for a temporary power boost.

games.name: Ra.One: The Game
games.description: A tie-in game to the Bollywood film Ra.One, where you fight through one-on-one duels and mob battles as characters from the movie, chaining melee combos and firing ranged weapons. It has Story, Battle, and Challenge modes, and supports up to four players at once.

games.name: Street Cricket Champions
games.description: An Indian-made arcade cricket game built around gully cricket, the informal street cricket played in Indian neighborhoods, rather than official international matches. You play fast four-a-side games in local settings like alleys and rooftops, picking from Indian characters or creating your own custom player.

games.name: Cart Kings
games.description: An India-specific kart racer where you race bullock carts, not go-karts, as characters licensed from the Tinkle and Amar Chitra Katha comic magazines. Tracks wind through Indian streets and past famous monuments, with pickups and power-ups in the mold of a Mario Kart style racer.

games.name: God of War
games.description: You play Kratos, a Spartan warrior wielding chained blades, hacking through hordes of mythological monsters across Greece while solving environmental puzzles and pulling levers to open new areas. Combat blends fast weapon combos, magic attacks, and context-sensitive finishing moves on weakened enemies.

games.name: God of War II
games.description: The direct sequel, again playing as Kratos, now stripped of his godhood and hunting the Fates to undo his fate, fighting bigger set-piece monsters like the Colossus of Rhodes and riding on the backs of giant creatures mid-battle.

games.name: Downhill Domination
games.description: A downhill mountain bike racing game where you bomb down steep courses at high speed, weaving between trees and rivals while doing tricks off jumps for extra boost. You can also shove, punch, or sabotage opponents mid-race to knock them off their bikes.

games.name: WWE SmackDown! Here Comes the Pain
games.description: A wrestling game built around the SmackDown! roster of the era, where you grapple, run the ropes, and pull off signature and finishing moves in singles, tag, and various gimmick matches like Hell in a Cell or Elimination Chamber. Season mode lets you build a wrestler's storyline over time.

games.name: Mortal Kombat: Shaolin Monks
games.description: Instead of one-on-one fighting, you control Liu Kang or Kung Lao through a linear action-adventure retelling the story of Mortal Kombat II, brawling through crowds of enemies with the series' signature fatalities and combo strings.

games.name: Burnout 3: Takedown
games.description: An arcade racer where the goal is as much about wrecking opponents as finishing first, ramming rivals off the road for a Takedown while boosting through oncoming traffic. Crash mode has you deliberately smash into busy intersections to cause the biggest, most destructive pileup possible.

games.name: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
games.description: You play the Prince, armed with a dagger that can rewind time, running along walls, leaping between pillars, and swinging across chasms inside an ornate Persian palace overrun by sand-transformed monsters. Combat mixes acrobatic sword fighting with the dagger's time-freeze and rewind powers.

games.name: Ben 10: Protector of Earth
games.description: You play Ben Tennyson, using the Omnitrix to transform into different alien forms, each with its own attacks and abilities, to fight enemies and solve simple puzzles across themed levels. Switching aliens mid-level is the core mechanic.

games.name: Ben 10: Alien Force
games.description: Set five years after the original series, you again play an older Ben switching between alien transformations to fight through levels, rescue his grandfather Max, and stop an invasion by the Highbreed.

games.name: Ben 10 Ultimate Alien: Cosmic Destruction
games.description: You play Ben using the Ultimatrix to access up-close alien forms and their more powerful 'Ultimate' evolutions mid-battle, brawling through enemies while traveling the world to recover pieces of an ancient artifact.

games.name: WWE All Stars
games.description: An over-the-top, exaggerated take on wrestling that pits current and legendary WWE superstars against each other with cartoonishly oversized moves, huge jumps, and impossible physics rather than simulation-style grappling.

games.name: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3
games.description: You skate through open, trick-filled levels doing grinds, flips, and grabs to chain together combos before time runs out, completing goals like collecting letters or hitting hidden gaps. A revert mechanic let you link ground tricks after landing rail or ramp combos.

games.name: The Incredibles: Rise of the Underminer
games.description: An alternate sequel picking up right where the 2004 film ended, as the Underminer drills out of the ground and declares war on the surface world. You play as Mr. Incredible or Frozone (swappable on the fly, with two-player co-op support), smashing through waves of the Underminer's robot minions with super strength or ice powers while the rest of the family handles other threats.

games.name: Spider-Man: Friend or Foe
games.description: A co-op focused brawler where Spider-Man teams up with a rotating cast of allies and former villains to fight waves of enemies across simple side-scrolling-style levels. You punch, web-swing, and use special moves while collecting alien tech crystals to unlock sidekicks.

games.name: Batman: Rise of Sin Tzu
games.description: A straightforward side-scrolling brawler where you pick Batman, Robin, Nightwing, or Batgirl and punch through waves of thugs across Gotham City locations, occasionally teaming up in two-player co-op, unlocking new gadgets and abilities as you progress.

games.name: LEGO Indiana Jones: The Original Adventures
games.description: You play through LEGO versions of the first three Indiana Jones films, smashing everything in sight to collect LEGO studs, solving simple physical puzzles, and swapping between characters with different abilities like whip-swinging or digging.

games.name: MotoGP 08
games.description: A motorcycle racing game based on the real MotoGP championship, where you race officially licensed riders and bikes around real-world circuits, managing lean angles, braking, and corner speed to avoid low-siding.

games.name: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer
games.description: A movie tie-in where you control members of the Fantastic Four, switching between characters to use their individual powers - stretching, force fields, flight and fire, and super strength - to fight enemies and solve simple co-op-style puzzles.

games.name: Super Smash Bros.
games.description: You pick a Nintendo character and fight on stage, but instead of a health bar you build up a damage percentage that makes opponents fly farther when hit, trying to knock them off the stage entirely. Items spawn randomly on stages themed after different Nintendo games.

games.name: Mario Kart 64
games.description: You race go-karts as Mario characters across fantastical tracks, drifting around corners and using item boxes that give shells, bananas, and mushrooms to attack rivals or boost your own speed. Battle mode pits players against each other in enclosed arenas instead of racing.

games.name: Pokémon Stadium 2
games.description: You bring Pokemon from the Game Boy games into 3D battles on the N64, picking teams and using moves in turn-based fights rendered with full animations instead of the handheld games' sprites. It also includes a large collection of minigames playable with up to four players.

games.name: Mario Party 3
games.description: Up to four players move around a board game map rolling dice blocks, buying stars, and landing on spaces that trigger random events, then play a minigame against each other at the end of every turn. It added a story mode and a Duel Mode board on top of the standard party format.

games.name: Shadowgun: DeadZone
games.description: You control a soldier in a sci-fi arena shooter, running and gunning through corridors and open combat zones against other players online. Matches revolve around picking up weapon and armor drops and using cover while your character auto-aims toward nearby enemies.

games.name: Club Penguin
games.description: You waddle around a snowy virtual world as a customizable penguin avatar, chatting with other kids, playing simple arcade minigames for coins, and decorating your igloo with furniture you buy. There's no single goal beyond hanging out, completing seasonal events, and collecting outfits and pins hidden around the island.

games.name: Dragon City
games.description: You build and manage a floating island city full of habitats, breeding pairs of dragons together to hatch new elemental types and feeding them until they're grown. You send your dragons into simple turn-based battles against other players' islands and spend most of your time expanding your city.

games.name: Social Wars
games.description: A classic real-time strategy and action game originally popularized on Facebook, where you build up a base, train troops, and manage resources in real time before sending your forces out to attack enemy camps or raid other players' bases. You recruit real Facebook friends to help defend or reinforce your army, with combat playing out as forces clash directly on the map rather than through pure menu-based simulation.

games.name: Social Empires
games.description: You develop a medieval-fantasy village on Facebook, placing buildings, farming resources, and training an army of units and heroes. Once your forces are ready you send them out on the map to clear enemy camps or raid other players' villages for loot.

games.name: Mutants: Genetic Gladiators
games.description: You splice together DNA from different species to create your own roster of mutant fighters, then send them into quick turn-based arena battles against other players' creations. Between fights you manage a lab, unlock new mutant parts, and level up your creatures.

games.name: Criminal Case
games.description: You play a police detective combing through cluttered crime scene images to find a checklist of hidden objects and clues, then use those clues to question suspects and narrow down a murderer. Each case unfolds over several scenes with a small cast of suspects.

games.name: Fleabag VS Mutt
games.description: A two-character throwing game where a cat (Fleabag) and a dog (Mutt) stand on opposite sides of a fence lobbing bones and cans at each other. You click and hold to charge your throw's power, release to fire, and have to account for shifting wind.

games.name: Skull Kid
games.description: You play a small kid with a demonic skull for a head, fighting your way up through the floors of an office tower armed with a chainsaw and other makeshift weapons, smashing furniture, computers, and anyone in your way. It's a short, violent rampage rather than a full platformer, played over a looping instrumental of 10cc's 'Dreadlock Holiday.' One of the most-played games ever hosted on Newgrounds.

games.name: Age of War
games.description: You defend a single lane against waves of enemy units, spending points to build your own troops and evolve your civilization from cavemen up through modern and futuristic technology. Each new age unlocks stronger units and a bigger special attack.

games.name: Age of War 2
games.description: A sequel that keeps the same lane-pushing formula of spawning troops and advancing through eras of technology, but adds new units, base upgrades, and balance changes over the original.

games.name: Cactus McCoy and the Curse of Thorns
games.description: You play a cowboy cursed to look like a walking cactus, hacking through outlaws and creatures with a sword across side-scrolling Old West levels. Levels mix platforming, treasure hunting, and beat-'em-up combat, with coins and gems to collect along the way.

games.name: Cactus McCoy 2: The Ruins of Calavera
games.description: The sequel sends Cactus McCoy through jungle ruins and temples instead of desert towns, with the same side-scrolling sword-fighting and platforming as the original plus new enemies and secret areas.

games.name: Papa's Pizzeria
games.description: You run a pizza shop alone, taking customer orders, prepping and baking each pizza to their exact topping requests, then boxing and serving it before the next customer's patience runs out. You're graded on speed and accuracy at the end of each day.

games.name: Papa's Freezeria
games.description: You manage an island sundae shop, building customized frozen desserts layer by layer - ice cream flavors, syrups, toppings, and whipped cream - to match each customer's order exactly. It's timed and station-based, with tips and grades depending on speed and accuracy.

games.name: Miami Shark
games.description: You control a great white shark swimming through a beach resort, leaping out of the water to grab and eat swimmers, jet skiers, and beachgoers for points. Between bites you upgrade your shark's abilities and unlock new areas and shark types.

games.name: Learn to Fly
games.description: You play a penguin trying to prove he can fly by launching himself off a ski-jump ramp, gliding as far as possible before crashing. Between attempts you spend the cash earned from distance on upgrades like better wings, boosters, and gliders.

games.name: Fireboy and Watergirl in the Forest Temple
games.description: You control two characters at once - a fire boy who can't touch water and a water girl who can't touch fire - guiding them together through a temple full of levers, elevators, and hazard pools using separate controls. Both characters need to reach their matching colored door at the same time.

games.name: Electric Man 2
games.description: A stick-figure fighting game where you battle a series of electric-powered opponents in one-on-one matches, using combos of punches, kicks, and special electric attacks to drain their health bar. You fight your way up through a tournament ladder of increasingly tough characters.

games.name: Bartender: The Right Mix
games.description: You work behind a bar taking drink orders and mixing them by clicking through the steps for each cocktail before the customer's patience meter runs out. Speed and accuracy earn tips, and the pace ramps up as more customers line up.

games.name: Superfighters
games.description: A side-scrolling brawler where stick-like fighters punch, kick, and grab weapons scattered around small arenas to knock each other out. It's built around local multiplayer on one keyboard, with physics-based melee combat, guns, and environmental hazards like explosive barrels.

games.name: Ben 10: Kraken Attack
games.description: Tied to the episode 'The Krakken,' you run in circles around a lake as one of Ben's aliens, shooting at a sea monster surfacing from the water while dodging its plasma attacks. The goal is to wear down its energy bar before time runs out.

games.name: Ben 10: Battle Ready
games.description: The first official Ben 10 web game, set at an abandoned factory where Ben trains with the Omnitrix through a series of combat challenges. You switch between all ten of Ben's classic alien forms, each with its own attacks, to fight off Vilgax's robot drones.

games.name: Ben 10: Big Battle
games.description: Loosely based on the 'Ben 10 vs. the Negative 10' special, you fight through side-scrolling levels as Ben's aliens, using arrow keys to move, a power button for special alien abilities, and separate jump/attack keys, collecting extra lives and energy tokens.

games.name: Ben 10: Ultimate Defense
games.description: Tied to the Ben 10: Ultimate Alien series, you defend the Rustbucket II by placing turrets based on Ben's Ultimate alien forms along a path to stop waves of enemies from reaching it.

games.name: Ben 10: Humungousaur Giant Force
games.description: Set in the Ben 10: Alien Force era, you play as the giant alien Humungousaur smashing through waves of DNAliens across six levels, punching and throwing enemy vehicles into obstacles.

games.name: Ben 10/Generator Rex: Heroes United
games.description: A crossover browser game tied to the TV special where Ben Tennyson and Rex Salazar team up after their two worlds collide. You play through action-adventure levels as one or both heroes, fighting enemies with Ben's alien transformations and Rex's mechanical powers.

games.name: LEGO: The Robot Chronicles
games.description: You drive around a version of LEGO City doing missions for various minifigure characters to stop Doctor Inferno's robot takeover. Tasks mix simple driving errands, races against other cars, and combat run-ins with Inferno's robots, unlocking new vehicles as you go.

games.name: Scooby-Doo: Horror on the High Seas
games.description: You guide Scooby and Shaggy through a haunted cruise ship and undersea caves across four short chapters, clicking through simple puzzles and platform-style bits to escape a ghost pirate who attacked their ship.

games.name: Scooby-Doo: Mayan Monster Mayhem
games.description: Scooby and Shaggy fly to Central America chasing the world's hottest pepper for a chili recipe, and you play through separate episodes like river rapids, a collapsing cave, and Mayan ruins, each its own small minigame strung together into one story.

games.name: Scooby-Doo: Haunts for the Holidays
games.description: A three-part holiday special where you control Scooby and Shaggy in London chasing the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Future through a theatre, a cellar, and a final showdown.

games.name: Tyrannosaurus Run
games.description: You drive a jeep at full speed down a jungle track while a giant T-Rex chases behind you, watching it loom closer in the rearview mirror as you dodge obstacles and grab crystals along the way.

games.name: Air Assault 2
games.description: You pilot one of several helicopters through desert, sea, and industrial levels, blasting ground troops, vehicles, and enemy aircraft with an arsenal of upgradable weapons and missiles. Boss fights cap off each stretch of levels, and money earned from missions buys better weapons and unlocks new choppers.

games.name: Total Overdose: A Gunslinger's Tale in Mexico
games.description: You play Ramiro Cruz, a DEA agent turned vigilante shooting his way through Mexican drug cartels to find out who killed his father. Combat revolves around diving in slow motion while dual-wielding pistols and pulling off over-the-top wrestling-style melee finishers.

games.name: Need for Speed: Most Wanted
games.description: You build a street-racing career in the open city of Rockport, racing named rivals up the Blacklist while a persistent, aggressive police force chases you with roadblocks, spike strips, and helicopters.

games.name: Snowy: Lunch Rush
games.description: Snowy the polar bear runs his own restaurant, and you seat customers, take their orders, cook and serve food, then collect payment and clear tables as fast as possible, using profits to upgrade to bigger locations.

games.name: Snowy: Treasure Hunter
games.description: You guide Snowy the bear through Egyptian, Greek, and Norse-themed side-view levels, digging through special blocks with his pickaxe and climbing ladders or ropes to collect every piece of treasure on screen. Snowy can't jump on his own, so reaching loot means carefully planning routes.

games.name: Snowy: Treasure Hunter 2
games.description: The sequel sends Snowy the bear treasure-hunting through Africa, the Mayan jungle, and the Far East, again digging and climbing through side-view levels to grab every bit of loot while avoiding apes, ancient guardians, and dragons.

games.name: Feeding Frenzy
games.description: You control a small fish that eats anything smaller than itself to grow bigger, while staying clear of larger fish that can eat you in turn. As you grow you move up through several tiers of sea life until you become one of the biggest predators in the level.

games.name: Feeding Frenzy 2: Shipwreck Showdown
games.description: The sequel keeps the eat-things-smaller-than-you gameplay but adds exploring sunken shipwrecks, more varied sea creatures to grow into, and larger boss encounters at the end of areas.

games.name: Farm Frenzy 2
games.description: You run a farm, collecting eggs, wool, milk, and other goods from animals, feeding them to keep production going, then converting raw goods into higher-value products in an on-screen factory to sell for profit before the level's timer runs out.

games.name: Money Movers
games.description: You control two brothers at once (or with a friend on the same keyboard) trying to reach the exit of each heist-themed level. The bigger brother is slow but can push and throw crates, while the smaller brother jumps higher and can dash through laser tripwires unharmed.

games.name: Snail Bob: Finding Home
games.description: Bob the snail moves forward on his own across a side-scrolling construction site full of hazards, and you click on him to make him stop and hide in his shell at the right moment, then click again to keep him moving, timing stops to dodge falling debris and machinery.

games.name: Bomb It 6
games.description: A Bomberman-inspired arena game for one or two players where you blow up destructible walls, grab power-ups, and try to catch opponents in the blast radius across several game modes. In this entry bombs periodically rain down from above, and you push them toward enemies before they go off.

games.name: My Dolphin Show
games.description: You control a trained dolphin performing tricks on command in a stadium show, timing jumps, flips, and ring dives to earn points and stars in short performance rounds.

games.name: Wheely 2
games.description: You help Wheely, a small cartoon car, get across each screen by clicking on objects to solve a simple physics puzzle - popping a tire, dropping a ramp, moving something out of the way. It continues the storyline of the original Wheely game.

games.name: Red Ball 3
games.description: You roll and jump a red ball through side-scrolling levels full of spikes, saws, and moving platforms, using physics-based momentum to clear gaps and reach the flag at the end of each stage.

games.name: RollerCoaster Creator
games.description: Using only your mouse, you draw a rollercoaster track from a starting station through hills, drops, and loops to a finish line, using different track tools at the bottom of the screen. The goal is to make the ride as thrilling as possible while picking up coins along the route.

games.name: Army of Ages
games.description: Your base sits on one side of the screen and an enemy base on the other, and you spend resources to spawn units that automatically march across the middle and fight when they meet, advancing through historical eras to unlock stronger units, in the same style as Age of War.

games.name: Sushi Cat: The Honeymoon
games.description: You drop a round cat character into pinball-like levels full of pegs, springs, and platforms, guiding its fall so it bumps into pieces of sushi that make it grow rounder and add to your score. Each level has a target weight or score to hit.

games.name: Knightmare Tower
games.description: You launch a knight upward by swinging his sword into enemies as he bounces off them, climbing an endless vertical tower and trying to reach as high as possible before gravity catches up and you fall. Coins collected during runs buy better swords, boots, and gear.

games.name: Zuma
games.description: You aim and fire colored balls from a stone frog statue into a chain of marbles snaking along a track, matching three or more of the same color to clear them before the chain reaches the skull carving at the end of the path.

games.name: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas
games.description: You play CJ, who returns to Los Santos after his mother's murder and gets pulled back into gang conflict across a large fictional state modeled on California and Nevada. You drive, shoot, and complete story missions across three cities and open countryside. Year shown is the PC release specifically (the original PS2 version released in 2004).

games.name: Blades of Brim
games.description: You dash forward automatically through a cartoon fantasy world, swiping to jump, slide, and knock away goblins and other enemies while collecting coins and gems. Along the way you unlock and ride different mounts and companions that change how you move and fight. Made by the Subway Surfers team, it swaps that game's city setting for a medieval fantasy one with knights and dragons.

games.name: Bowmasters
games.description: You pick one of dozens of goofy characters and take turns flinging a weapon - arrows, axes, grenades, and stranger things - at an opponent standing across the screen, pulling back and releasing to aim with gravity affecting the shot. A solid hit knocks the other character out in an exaggerated, cartoonishly gory way. New characters and weapons are unlocked with coins earned from matches and mini-games.

games.name: N.O.V.A. Legacy
games.description: A slimmed-down, free-to-play reboot of Gameloft's N.O.V.A. series, putting you in space marine armor to shoot through alien-infested corridors and open areas in a single-player campaign styled after console sci-fi shooters. It also has online multiplayer deathmatches for up to six players, plus a gear-upgrade system for weapons and suits.

games.name: Marvel: Future Fight
games.description: You collect and level up a roster of Marvel heroes and villains, forming teams of three to fight through story missions, raid bosses, and PvP arenas using tap-and-swipe combat with special abilities. Characters are upgraded with gear, ISO-8 crystals, and uniforms tied to different stories from the comics and films.

games.name: Stickman Legends: Offline Games
games.description: You control a stickman ninja fighting through side-view levels and arena battles, swinging swords and other melee weapons in fast combo-based combat against waves of enemies and bosses. It has an offline story campaign plus PvP arena modes, with gear and skill upgrades earned from fights used to unlock new stickman heroes and weapon types. Developed by Zitga, part of a large family of stickman fighting games from the same studio.

games.name: PUBG Mobile
games.description: You parachute onto a large island along with up to 99 other players, scavenge for weapons, armor, and vehicles, and fight to be the last one standing as a shrinking safe zone forces everyone closer together. Matches can be played solo, in duos, or in squads, with touchscreen controls for aiming, looting, and driving.

games.name: Doodle Army 2: Mini Militia
games.description: A side-scrolling, doodle-drawn shooter where you run, jump, and use jetpack boots to fly around small maps while picking up guns, shotguns, and rocket launchers scattered on the ground. Matches support both local and online multiplayer for up to six players, in modes like team deathmatch and capture the flag.

games.name: Paper.io 2
games.description: You control a small colored square that trails a line behind it as you move around an open arena, and closing a loop claims that area as your territory. Other players are doing the same thing at the same time, and running into anyone's trail (including your own tail) eliminates you.

games.name: Snake.io
games.description: A multiplayer take on the classic Snake formula: you steer a growing snake around an open arena, eating glowing pellets to get longer while trying to cut off other players so they crash into your body. Crashing into another snake or the edge ends your run and drops your length as pellets for others to eat.

games.name: Hide Online: Prop Hunt
games.description: A team-based hide-and-seek game where one side disguises themselves as ordinary objects scattered around the map - a chair, a crate, a vending machine - and tries to avoid detection, while armed hunters search the area and shoot anything suspicious before time runs out. You switch roles between rounds.

games.name: Mmm Fingers
games.description: You place one finger on the screen and hold it there as long as you can while cartoon mouths, teeth, and other hazards close in from the edges, trying to bite down on you. The only input is holding still or carefully repositioning without ever lifting off the screen, and the run ends the instant something chomps you. Score is purely how long you survive, with leaderboards to compare against friends.

games.name: Crash of Cars
games.description: You drive one of many unlockable toy-like vehicles around a small arena, ramming, shooting, and knocking other players' cars off the edge or into hazards in real-time online matches. Power-ups scattered around the map give temporary weapons or boosts.

games.name: Jetpack Joyride
games.description: You control Barry Steakfries as he runs automatically through a lab, wearing a jetpack you tap to fly up and release to fall, dodging laser trip-wires, missiles, and zappers while collecting coins. Coins unlock a large variety of different jetpacks and gadgets with unique flight behaviors.

games.name: Hungry Dragon
games.description: You control a dragon flying and swimming through an open world, eating everything in sight - sheep, fish, cars, people - to grow bigger and rack up points before something bigger eats or kills you. Different dragons have different abilities like fire breath or ice attacks.

games.name: Smash Hit
games.description: You move through a stylized, minimalist 3D world by throwing metal balls to smash glass obstacles blocking your path, timing your throws as the camera glides forward on its own. Running out of balls or hitting an obstacle ends your run, but crystals scattered through each stage replenish your ammo.

games.name: Mortal Kombat Mobile
games.genre: Fighting game with card-collection RPG elements
games.description: You collect cards representing Mortal Kombat characters and use them in teams of three to fight in tap-and-swipe battles, chaining basic attacks, special moves, and fatality-style finishers. Characters are leveled up and equipped through a gacha-style card system, with story chapters, faction wars, and PvP towers.

games.name: Mr Bullet - Spy Puzzles
games.description: In each short level you aim and fire a pistol at a lineup of enemies, using ricochets off walls and precise angles to take everyone out with a limited number of bullets. Levels get more elaborate with moving targets, hostages you have to avoid hitting, and environmental gimmicks that change how shots bounce.

games.name: Plants vs. Zombies 2: It's About Time
games.description: You plant a row of defensive plants across your lawn to stop waves of zombies from reaching your house, using sun currency collected over time or from sunflowers to unlock more plants as a level goes on. Different worlds are themed around different time periods like Ancient Egypt and the Wild West.

games.name: DUAL!
games.description: A minimalist two-player game where each person holds one phone, and the two screens act as opposite sides of the same playing field connected over Bluetooth or Wi-Fi. In the main mode you tilt your phone to dodge incoming shots and tap to charge and fire back at your opponent's screen.

games.name: Planet Bomber!
games.description: You tap the screen to launch missiles and bombs down at a rotating planet, trying to crack through its layers and destroy its core while dodging or clearing bosses that appear partway through. Currency earned from blowing up planets is spent on stronger weapons.

games.name: Gangstar New Orleans
games.description: A Grand Theft Auto-style open-world game where you drive vehicles, shoot enemies, and complete story missions across a version of New Orleans as you build up a criminal empire. You can free-roam the city causing chaos and upgrade weapons, cars, and your safehouse over time.

games.name: Among Us
games.description: You play as a crewmate (or, secretly, an impostor) aboard a spaceship or other setting, completing simple task minigames while trying to figure out who among the group is secretly killing people off. Impostors sabotage systems and eliminate crewmates while blending in.

games.name: Fleet Battle: Sea Battle PvP
games.description: You play the classic Battleship board game against another player, taking turns firing at grid coordinates to sink each other's hidden fleet before yours is destroyed. You collect and customize fleets from different real-world navies, playing offline against the computer or online in ranked PvP matches.

games.name: Injustice 2
games.description: You collect and upgrade cards representing DC heroes and villains, then use teams of three in tap-based fights that mirror moves and combos from the console Injustice 2 fighting game. Progression is driven by gearing up characters, leveling shards, and grinding through story chapters and PvP arenas.

games.name: Storyteller
games.description: You arrange characters and settings within panels of a wordless comic-book layout to make a short story play out correctly, dragging in figures like a knight, a vampire, or a princess and moving them between rooms to trigger different outcomes. Each puzzle gives you a title and you rearrange the panels until the sequence matches it.

games.name: Wuthering Waves
games.description: You control a small squad of characters pulled from gacha banners, switching between them in real time to chain elemental attacks and dodges against monsters called Tacet Discords. It's an open-world game where you explore a windswept, semi-post-apocalyptic setting, run story quests, and farm materials to level up characters and weapons.

games.name: Marvel Contest of Champions
games.description: You collect Marvel heroes and villains pulled from crystals and pit them against each other in one-on-one fights using tap and swipe combos, blocking, and special abilities. Progress runs through story chapters called Acts, timed arena events, and versus battles against other players' rosters.

games.name: Stumble Guys
games.description: Up to 32 players race through obstacle-course levels at once, dodging swinging hammers, spinning platforms, and each other while trying to reach the finish line. Each round eliminates stragglers until a final map decides the winner.

games.name: Free Fire
games.description: Up to 50 players parachute onto an island, scavenge weapons and gear, and fight until one player or squad is left standing inside a shrinking safe zone. Matches are shorter than most battle royale games, and before dropping in you pick a character with a passive special ability.

games.name: Drive Ahead!
games.description: A two-player (or vs-CPU) game where you drive cars around small arenas trying to launch the other vehicle's driver head off using ramps, hazards, and momentum. Rounds are short and chaotic, won by knocking your opponent off the platform or popping their driver off.

games.name: Battlegrounds Mobile India (BGMI)
games.description: The India-specific version of PUBG Mobile, where up to 100 players parachute onto a large island, loot weapons and supplies, and fight to be the last one standing as the safe zone shrinks. Maps, weapons, and progression are essentially identical to PUBG Mobile.

games.name: Dragon Ball Legends
games.description: A mobile fighting game built around Dragon Ball characters pulled from gacha summons, where you tap cards to unleash basic attacks, ki blasts, and special moves in real-time battles. You build a team of three characters and manage a rock-paper-scissors-style card system.

games.name: Asphalt 8: Airborne
games.description: An arcade racer where you drive licensed sports cars through checkpoint tracks, pulling barrel rolls and big jumps off ramps to fill a nitro boost meter. You collect and upgrade cars, race against AI or other players, and unlock new events and tracks.

games.name: Tank Stars
games.description: Two tanks take turns aiming and firing an arsenal of missiles, bombs, and gimmick weapons across a 2D destructible battlefield, trying to knock the other tank's health to zero. You unlock new tanks and weapons as you clear campaign stages or play online.

games.name: Clash of Clans
games.description: You build and upgrade a village of buildings, defenses, and resource generators, then train troops to raid other players' villages for loot. You also join a Clan to donate troops, fight in clan wars, and defend your own base from incoming attacks.

games.name: Clash Royale
games.description: A head-to-head strategy game where you deploy cards representing troops, spells, and buildings from the Clash of Clans universe, trying to destroy your opponent's towers before they destroy yours in a short timed match.

games.name: Solo Leveling: Arise
games.description: A gacha action RPG based on the Solo Leveling web novel/manhwa, where you play as Sung Jinwoo, summoning shadow soldiers from defeated enemies to fight beside you in real-time hack-and-slash combat. You dungeon-crawl through story missions and grind gear and shadows to get stronger.

games.name: Dead Trigger 2
games.description: A first-person shooter where you complete short missions across various locations, gunning down waves of infected with an arsenal of unlockable, upgradable weapons. Between missions you manage a base, research new equipment, and take on special limited-time events.

games.name: Where's My Water?
games.description: You drag your finger to dig or clear paths through dirt so that water flows down and fills an alligator's bathtub or otherwise reaches a goal. Later levels add other liquids like ooze and poison gas, using flow and momentum as the core puzzle mechanic.

games.name: Adventure Beaks
games.description: A side-scrolling auto-runner where a squad of penguins moves forward automatically while you tap and swipe to jump, slide, and dive around obstacles and enemies across snow, jungle, and underwater levels. You collect currency to dress your penguins in hats, jackets, and other cosmetics.

games.name: Critical Ops
games.description: A competitive first-person shooter modeled on Counter-Strike, where two teams complete objective-based rounds like bomb defusal on small maps, buying weapons and gear with in-game currency between rounds. Played with virtual touch controls.

games.name: Forward Assault
games.description: A multiplayer first-person shooter structured like Counter-Strike, where teams face off in round-based matches such as bomb defusal or team deathmatch on small maps, buying weapons at the start of each round. Played with on-screen touch controls.

games.name: Football Strike
games.description: A simplified arcade soccer game centered on shooting sequences, where you flick the ball past a goalkeeper in penalty-shootout-style matches, plus multiplayer 1v1 and head-to-head modes. You unlock player characters, balls, and stadiums.

games.name: Stormblades
games.description: A hack-and-slash game where you control a warrior fighting through arena-style waves of monsters using directional sword swipes, similar in feel to Infinity Blade. Between fights you upgrade your sword and infuse it with elemental powers.

games.name: Vector
games.description: A parkour-style runner where you control a silhouetted figure fleeing pursuers, swiping to vault over obstacles, slide under barriers, wall-run, and climb across rooftops and industrial settings. Levels are timed courses rather than a pure endless mode.

games.name: Shadow Fight 2
games.description: A side-view fighting game where your silhouette character fights a series of increasingly tough opponents using martial arts combos of punches, kicks, and weapons, with gear you find changing your stats and appearance. You progress through a story-driven campaign across different gates.

games.name: Warlings: Armageddon
games.description: A turn-based strategy game modeled closely on Worms, where you control a small squad of characters taking turns to move and fire one weapon each across a 2D destructible battlefield, trying to eliminate the opposing team.

games.name: Temple Run
games.description: An endless runner where you flee down a jungle temple path pursued by monstrous creatures, swiping to turn, jump over gaps, slide under obstacles, and lean to collect coins along the way. The run ends when you crash, fall, or get caught.

games.name: Subway Surfers
games.description: An endless runner where you dash along train tracks as a graffiti-tagging character being chased by an inspector and his dog, swiping to dodge oncoming trains, jump over barriers, and slide under obstacles while collecting coins and power-ups.

games.name: Talking Tom Cat
games.description: A virtual pet app where you interact with an animated cat named Tom, who repeats back anything you say into the phone in a high-pitched voice and reacts to being poked, patted on the belly, or having his tail pulled. There's no goal or story, just talking to him and recording clips.

games.name: Fruit Ninja
games.description: You swipe your finger across the touchscreen like a blade to slice flying fruit that gets tossed up from the bottom of the screen. Bananas, watermelons, and pineapples fly through the air and you cut them into pieces for points and combos, while black bombs must be avoided or they end the round.

games.name: Cut the Rope
games.description: Each level presents a small green creature named Om Nom and a piece of candy dangling from ropes, bubbles, or pegs. You slice the ropes with your finger, timing swings and bounces so the candy drops into Om Nom's mouth, while also collecting three stars along the way and avoiding spikes and fire.

games.name: NinJump
games.description: You play a ninja bouncing back and forth between two walls, climbing ever higher as the screen scrolls. Tapping or swiping makes the ninja jump from one wall to the other, and you have to dodge birds, saw blades, and other obstacles in the way.

games.name: Hill Climb Racing
games.description: You drive a cartoonish vehicle across bumpy, hilly 2D terrain using just two buttons for gas and brake/reverse, trying to keep your driver's head from smacking the ground while collecting coins and fuel canisters. Different vehicles and tracks unlock as you earn coins to upgrade engine, suspension, and tires.

games.name: Pixel Gun 3D
games.description: A blocky, Minecraft-styled first-person shooter with a single-player campaign against zombies and other creatures, plus online multiplayer modes like deathmatch, team battles, and battle royale. You earn coins and gems to unlock new weapons and customize your character's skin.

games.name: World Robot Boxing
games.description: You control a giant robot boxer in one-on-one matches, tapping and swiping to throw jabs, hooks, and uppercuts while blocking and dodging your opponent's punches. You fight through a series of career-mode opponents, earning currency to repair, upgrade, and unlock entirely new robots.

games.name: Transformers: Earth Wars
games.description: You pick a side, Autobots or Decepticons, and build up a base with resource generators, defenses, and a barracks of troops that can switch between vehicle and robot forms. You send out squads to raid other players' bases while defending your own, and can join an alliance for larger wars.

games.name: Rodeo Stampede: Sky Zoo Safari
games.description: Your character automatically runs forward across floating chunks of savanna, and you leap from the back of one wild animal onto the next by tapping at the right moment, riding zebras, rhinos, and eventually much stranger creatures. Tamed animals get added to a sky-borne zoo you can visit between runs.

games.name: Burrito Bison: Launcha Libre
games.description: You launch a luchador-wrestler character out of a slingshot into a field of gummy bears, bouncing off them to build up speed and distance across a side-scrolling stage. Along the way you smash gold-filled pinatas and collect coins to spend on upgrades between runs.

games.name: Dr. Driving
games.description: You drive a car from a top-down/behind view through traffic-filled courses, following basic road rules like stopping at red lights and staying in your lane while completing objectives such as parking or reaching a destination within a time limit. Missions are scored, unlocking new cars and modes.

games.name: Traffic Rider
games.description: You ride a motorcycle from a first-person, over-the-handlebars viewpoint, weaving through lanes of oncoming and same-direction traffic on a highway to earn points for close calls and overtakes. There's a career mode with story-driven missions plus an endless mode for high scores.

games.name: Spider-Man Unlimited
games.description: You swing, dash, and slide as Spider-Man through New York streets and rooftops in an endless-runner format, tapping to jump/attack and swiping to dodge obstacles and enemies. Waves of classic villains appear as bosses, and you collect alternate-universe Spider-Man variants to add to your roster.

games.name: Unicorn Dash: Magical Run
games.description: You control a unicorn that runs forward automatically through fantasy environments like enchanted forests, rainbows, and clouds. You tap to jump over gaps and obstacles and use a dash/flight power-up to boost past danger, collecting coins and gems to unlock new unicorn characters.

games.name: Pitfall!
games.description: This 2012 mobile reboot turns the classic jungle-exploring character Pitfall Harry into an endless-runner/platformer hybrid. You swipe to make Harry run, jump, slide, and swing on vines through jungle and temple environments, dodging pits, rolling logs, and native traps while grabbing gold and treasure.

games.name: Angry Birds
games.description: You pull back a slingshot and launch different birds at ramshackle pig fortresses made of wood, stone, ice, and glass, trying to destroy the structures and pop every pig on the screen. Each bird species has its own special ability you can trigger mid-flight.

games.name: Angry Birds Space
games.description: The same slingshot-and-destroy gameplay as the original Angry Birds, but set among planets and asteroids in zero gravity, so your shots curve and loop around the gravity wells of nearby planetoids instead of flying in a simple arc.

games.name: Angry Birds Star Wars
games.description: The familiar slingshot destruction gameplay reskinned with a Star Wars theme: birds are dressed as characters like Luke Skywalker and Han Solo, each with a themed special power, while pigs play the roles of Imperial troops and Darth Vader. Levels loosely follow the original trilogy's plot.

games.name: Angry Birds Go!
games.description: This spin-off turns the Angry Birds cast into a kart racing game: your bird or pig character races downhill along winding tracks, using tilt or touch steering, boosting, and drifting, while collecting coins and power-up items scattered along the road.

games.name: Angry Birds Epic
games.description: This spin-off drops the slingshot entirely in favor of turn-based combat: your birds, each assigned a class like Warrior, Mage, or Rogue, take turns tapping enemy pigs to attack while managing special abilities and items during fights. Story missions culminate in boss fights against King Pig's forces.

games.name: Angry Birds Stella
games.description: This spin-off keeps the core slingshot-and-destroy physics puzzle gameplay of the original games but stars Stella and her group of friends on a setting called Golden Island, each with a somewhat different special power than the mainline birds.

games.name: Angry Birds Transformers
games.description: This spin-off recasts the Angry Birds cast as Transformers-style robots that can shift between vehicle and robot forms. Levels run on rails: your character drives or flies forward automatically while you swipe to change lanes and tap to shoot Decepticon pig enemies, with boss fights at the end of stages.

games.name: Angry Birds Evolution
games.description: A team-based RPG where you collect and hatch a large roster of bird characters, each with rarity tiers, and can evolve them into stronger forms as you level up. You assemble squads of birds for largely automated battles against pig forces while exploring a map of islands, plus PvP arena matches.

games.name: Angry Birds 2
games.description: This sequel returns to the classic slingshot gameplay of launching birds at pig structures, but adds a Spell Card system you choose from before each level for one-time bonus effects, plus multi-room levels and periodic boss fights against pigs like King Pig.

games.name: War Robots
games.description: You pilot a giant robot in real-time 6-vs-6 online team battles across arena-style maps, using a movement joystick and weapon targeting to fight other players' mechs in third-person view. You unlock and collect different robot chassis and weapon loadouts, upgrading them with resources earned from matches.

games.name: Gangstar Vegas
games.description: A Grand Theft Auto-style open-world game set in a fictional Las Vegas, where you drive vehicles, shoot enemies, and pull off missions ranging from bank robberies to MMA-style fistfights and zombie shootouts. You free-roam the city between story missions, causing chaos, gambling, and street racing, while upgrading weapons, cars, and your character's stats along the way.
